It reproduces the shape that broke the capture: a Friday 2026-07-10 ARTI-7 merge-session burst (five messages ~25 min apart), a ~5-day (~125h) silence, then the Wednesday 2026-07-15 PR-comment anchor burst (two messages minutes before the reacted message, then the reacted message itself). This is the CHRONOLOGICAL order (anchor last) that get_context produces after messages.reverse() — feed it straight into _segment_context_by_gap. TO REPLACE WITH A REAL WINDOW (deferred; requires Plan A / ISSUES #14 serve seam to be running on 127.0.0.1:7777): curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $(cat ~/.local/state/slack-harvester/api-token)" 'http://127.0.0.1:7777/slack?method=conversations.history&channel=D0AUM6S6HQS&latest=1784148148.166149&limit=16&inclusive=true' | jq '{messages: (.messages | reverse)}' > tests/fixtures/rangel-window.real.json  (the .real.json name is gitignored so a real DM transcript never lands in git; the O1 test prefers rangel-window.real.json when present, else falls back to this synthetic file).
